User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PowerHub CRS platform.

role.description: The user's role within the platform (e.g., Resident, Seller, Service Provider, Driver).

rating.description: User rating, if applicable (e.g., for service providers).

profileImageUrl.description: URL of the user's profile image.

location.description: The user's current location, useful for proximity based services.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report submitted by a user regarding an issue (e.g., power outage, flood).

category.description: Category of the report (e.g., power, flood, waste).

coords.description: Geographical coordinates of the reported issue.

media.description: URL of the attached media (image, video) for the report.

status.description: Status of the report (e.g., pending, in progress, resolved).

upvotes.description: Number of upvotes the report has received.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the report was created.

description.description: Additional details provided by the user, such as the location or time of the incident.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered by a user (e.g., AgroConnect, SkillsHub, LaundryHub).

category.description: Category of the service (e.g., AgroConnect, SkillsHub).

location.description: Location where the service is offered.

contact.description: Contact information for the service provider.

description.description: A description of the service offered.

Area.title: Area
Area.description: Represents an area and its aggregated stats (e.g., power status, flood score, waste score).

powerStatus.description: Status of power in the area (e.g., ON, OFF).

floodScore.description: Flood risk score for the area.

wasteScore.description: Waste overflow score for the area.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction (e.g., bookings, pickups) between users and service providers.

userId.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction) User initiating the transaction (e.g., booking a service).

serviceId.description: Reference to Service. (Relationship: Service 1:N Transaction) The service that is being transacted.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the transaction occurred.

status.description: Status of the transaction (e.g., pending, completed, cancelled).

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the user can access their own data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores reports submitted by users. Publicly readable, write-protected to authenticated users.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the report.

definition.description: Stores service listings offered by users. Only the service owner can edit or delete their services.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the service.

definition.description: Stores aggregated stats for different areas. Publicly readable.

params.name: areaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the area.

definition.description: Stores transactions between users and service providers.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the PowerHub CRS application, focusing on user management, reporting, service marketplaces, and community alerts. It prioritizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability, aligning with the specified design principles. The structure employs path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative data. It also leverages structural segregation by keeping data that requires different security posture in different collections. 

Key structural choices and their justifications:

1.  **User Data (`/users/{userId}`):** Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership simplifies security rules, ensuring only the user can access their own data.

2.  **Reports (`/reports/{reportId}`):** Stores reports submitted by users.  Each document contains the `userId` to identify who created the report.  Publicly readable (QAP: Rules are not filters) but write-protected to authenticated users only.  The `reportId` is a UUID.

3.  **Services (`/services/{serviceId}`):** Stores service listings offered by users. Each service document includes the `ownerId` to denote the service provider. This path structure ensures that only the service owner can edit or delete their services.

4.  **Areas (`/areas/{areaId}`):** Stores aggregated stats for different areas.  Publicly readable.

5.  **Transactions (`/transactions/{transactionId}`):** Stores transactions between users and service providers.  Each transaction document contains the `userId` and `serviceId` to establish the relationship. Allows tracking transaction history and status.

This structure enables Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls. Access control is enforced directly on each document using the `request.auth.uid` and the denormalized `ownerId` or `userId` fields.  It also supports the required QAPs (Rules are not Filters). The `/reports` and `/areas` collections can be listed without filtering, as their security posture allows public read access.  Private user data is secured using path-based ownership.
